meaning: adj.肉的,肉体的,[植]肉质的,丰满的
examples: It seems like magic that a fleshy lump of gray, disgusting meat can give rise to these feelings.
He was well-built, but too fleshy to be impressive.
The standard epicurism person lets a person "fleshy mound".
Gregory (Fossilman) Raymer, a fleshy 39-year-old patent attorney for Pfizer, holds a pair of eights.
When Wiggins blistered 44 kilometers through these streets, grandmothers screamed his name, fake sideburns pasted to fleshy faces.
